congregation.def: 會眾、集會、聚集的人物 (單數)
congregation.ex: The large church congregation sang together.

congregational.def: 會眾的、公理教會的人的人物
congregational.ex: She enjoyed the lively congregational singing.

congress.def: 國會、議會、正式會議人物 (單數)
congress.col: library of congress, indian national congress, act of congress
congress.ex: The new law was passed by the Congress today.

congressman.def: 國會議員、眾議院議員人物
congressman.ex: The local congressman visited the school today.

congressional.def: 國會的、議會的人的人物
congressional.ex: He attended the important congressional hearing.

congruence.def: 適合、一致、全等的人物 (單數)
congruence.ex: There was a full congruence between the reports.

congruent.def: 適合的、一致的、全等的人的人物
congruent.ex: The two triangles are congruent on the map.

congruity.def: 適合、一致、協調的人物 (單數)
congruity.ex: Social congruity is important for the group.

conic.def: 圓錐的、圓錐形的人的人物 (單數)
conic.ex: He studied the conic section in math class.

conical.def: 圓錐形的、圓錐的人的人物 (錐)
conical.ex: The farmer wore a large and dry conical hat.

conifer.def: 松柏類植物、針葉樹人物 (單數)
conifer.ex: The mountain was covered with evergreen conifers.

coniferous.def: 松柏類的、接毬果的人的人物
coniferous.ex: They walked through the dark coniferous forest.

conjectural.def: 推測的、猜想的人的人物
conjectural.ex: The report was based on purely conjectural data.

conjecture.def: 推測、猜想、揣摩的人的人人物 (單數)
conjecture.ex: The true cause is still a matter of conjecture.

conjoin.def: 結合、連結、聯合的人的人
conjoin.ex: The two local groups decided to conjoin forces.

conjoint.def: 結合的、聯合的人的人 (或物)
conjoint.ex: They carried out a conjoint analysis of data.

conjugal.def: 婚姻的、結婚的人的人物
conjugal.ex: The prisoner was allowed a brief conjugal visit.

conjugate.def: 結合、(語)使動詞變化、結合的人的人物
conjugate.ex: The students learned how to conjugate French verbs.

conjugation.def: 結合、(語)動詞變化的人物 (單數)
conjugation.ex: Verb conjugation is a difficult part of grammar.

conjunction.def: 結合、(語)連接詞、同時發生人物 (單數)
conjunction.ex: We worked in conjunction with the local police.

conjunctiva.ex: The patient had a red and inflamed conjunctiva.

conjunctivitis.ex: He was sent home today with acute conjunctivitis.

conjuncture.def: 事態、關頭、結合的人物 (單數)
conjuncture.ex: The project reached a critical local conjuncture.

conjuration.def: 祝禱、咒語、招魂的人物 (單數)
conjuration.ex: The witch muttered an ancient dark conjuration.

conjure.def: 變戲法、念咒召喚、想起的人的人
conjure.col: conjure up (想起), name to conjure with (響亮名字)
conjure.ex: The smell managed to conjure up old memories.

conjurer/conjuror.def: 魔術師、變戲法者人物 (單數)
conjurer/conjuror.ex: The skilled conjurer pulled a rabbit from hat.

conjuring.ex: He showed us an amazing conjuring trick today.

conk.def: (口)鼻子、敲頭、拋錨的人物 (單數)
conk.col: conk out (故障、昏睡), big conk
conk.ex: The old car engine managed to conk out tonight.

connect.def: 連接、聯絡、聯繫、插上電源的人的人
connect.col: connect with, connect to, well connected
connect.ex: You need to connect to the local internet now.

connected.def: 連接的、有背景的人的人 (或物)
connected.ex: He is a very well connected person in the city.

connection.def: 連接、關係、親戚、轉接交通工具的人物 (單數)
connection.col: internet connection, emotional connection, miss a connection
connection.ex: I am afraid we might miss our train connection.

connective.def: 連接的、連結物的人物 (單數)
connective.ex: Connective tissue holds the body organs tonight.

connectivity.def: 連通性、連接的人物 (單數)
connectivity.ex: The hotel offers free broadband connectivity.

connector.ex: Please insert the metal connector into the port.

connivance.ex: He escaped with the connivance of the guard.

connive.def: 默許、共謀、縱容的人的人
connive.ex: The police were suspected to connive at crime.

connoisseur.ex: He is a real connoisseur of fine French wines.

connotative.def: 隱含的、內涵的人的人物
connotative.ex: The word has a very strong connotative meaning.

connote.def: 隱含、意味、內涵的人的人
connote.ex: Red color can often connote a feeling of danger.
